body.title: CLOSED: BaseDevice heartbeat — push liveness to comms://heartbeat every N seconds
body.result: BaseDevice.start_heartbeat() daemon thread publishing to comms://heartbeat, bd7fa43
body.description:
Add heartbeat publication to BaseDevice. Every live device publishes an envelope to comms://heartbeat every N seconds (default 30, config-driven). This IS the health monitoring mechanism — silence is the failure signal.

Heartbeat envelope:
  device_id, ts (ISO 8601), version, current_action, uptime_s,   plus any device-specific fields

Silence thresholds (config-driven):
  > 2N seconds — suspect (log warning)
  > 3N seconds — presumed down (flag to rack)

Complements (not replaces) the pull health() interface:
  heartbeat = continuous, lightweight, push — 'I exist'
  health()  = on-demand, rich, pull — 'tell me exactly how you are'

**Affected files:** agent_datacenter/device.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; heartbeat goes through bus, not direct connection
**Scope boundary:** publication only — collection/aggregation is T-librarian-health-aggregator
**Test plan:** device publishes heartbeat at configured interval; envelope contains required fields; stops publishing on halt()
